fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New York Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity new york, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to new york, pharmacy technician compact new york, new york pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: New York Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: New York State Education Department, Office of the Professions, P.O. Box 22000, Albany, NY 12201

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in New York.

steps.title: Review Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Visit the NYSED Office of the Professions Pharmacy Technician page (op.nysed.gov/professions/pharmacy-technician) to review all current eligibility criteria, including age, education, supervised experience, and examination requirements. Ensure you meet all prerequisites before proceeding.

steps.title: Download and Complete Application Form 1
steps.description: Download and accurately complete Form 1, 'Application for Licensure' for Pharmacy Technicians, from the NYSED Office of the Professions website. This form gathers your personal information and application details.

steps.title: Arrange for Supporting Documents (Forms 2 & 3)
steps.description: Arrange for your professional education program to complete and mail Form 2, 'Certification of Professional Education,' directly to NYSED. Similarly, arrange for your employer(s) to complete and mail Form 3, 'Certification of Supervised Experience,' directly to NYSED, if applicable, based on your pathway to licensure.

steps.title: Pass a Certification Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass one of the approved certification examinations: either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Exam (PTCE) administered by the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the ExCPT Exam administered by the National Healthcareer Association (NHA). Ensure your official scores are sent directly to the NYSED Office of the Professions.

steps.title: Submit Application Packet and Fee
steps.description: Mail your completed Form 1, along with a check or money order for the required licensure and first registration fee ($100), payable to the 'New York State Education Department,' to the mailing address for the NYSED Office of the Professions provided above.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status Online
steps.description: After submitting your application, create an account on My.NYSED.gov. This portal allows you to track the status of your application, review any deficiencies, and receive updates on your licensure process once your application has been received and processed by the department.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: New York offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the New York Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in New York by submitting a new application to the New York Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any New York-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a New York pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: New York charges a $75 application fee plus a $100 first registration fee for registered pharmacy technicians. The triennial renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New York have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to New York?
faqs.answer: The New York Office of the Professions asks applicants to allow six weeks before requesting a status update on a pharmacy technician application.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Contact the New York Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in New York.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in New York by submitting a new application to the New York Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any New York-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in New York
hero.dek: Apply for a Pharmacy Technician license in New York by submitting the required forms via mail to the NYSED Office of the Professions. Start by downloading Form 1 and other necessary documents from the Pharmacy Technician licensing page on the NYSED Office of the Professions website (op.nysed.gov/professions/pharmacy-technician).

variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

official_links.portal_name: NYSED Office of the Professions Licensing Information Portal
official_links.registration_steps: While the initial application for licensure is submitted by mail, applicants can create a My.NYSED.gov account to track their application status, register their license, and manage renewals. Visit My.NYSED.gov and select 'Don't have an account? Sign up now' to create an account.
